name: Century Savings And Loan Association
description_short: Mutual savings and loan chartered in 1903, offering FDIC-insured deposits and loans for residential and commercial real estate.
description_long:
Century Savings and Loan Association was established in 1903 as Century Building and Loan with a mission to provide share accounts and home mortgage financing to the local community. Over 120 years, it has evolved from a single-purpose lender into a diversified financial institution while maintaining its mutual structure—owned by depositors rather than external shareholders. The organization remains rooted in its founding principle of community service.

The institution offers a range of deposit products including share accounts, checking accounts, money market deposit accounts (MMDA), certificates of deposit (CDs), and individual retirement accounts (IRAs). On the lending side, they provide home mortgages, commercial real estate loans, agricultural real estate loans, and land purchase financing. Additional services include online banking, mobile banking, bill pay functionality, debit cards, wire transfers, and ATM access. All deposits are FDIC-insured.

Century Savings distinguishes itself through deep local ownership and multi-generational customer relationships. The organization emphasizes its mutual structure with local board members, staff longevity (many employees with 20+ years tenure), and branded commitment to being "Local People Helping Local People." They position themselves as having "stood the test of time" through customer loyalty spanning generations, with many current customers being descendants of original account holders.

As a traditional mutual savings and loan, Century Savings serves customers seeking local relationship banking and community-oriented financial services. However, prospective customers should note the website does not display current interest rates, loan terms, fees, or detailed product specifications. The organization operates limited physical locations and the website lacks pricing transparency common to modern online banks and competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
Mutual structure owned by depositors rather than external shareholders
Multi-generational local ownership with board members from the community
Staff retention and stability—many employees with 20+ years tenure
Comprehensive service suite including online banking, mobile app, and bill pay
Diverse lending products spanning residential mortgages, commercial real estate, agricultural loans, and land purchases
Long institutional history (chartered 1903) demonstrating stability and longevity
cons: Website does not display current interest rates, APRs, or loan terms
Limited branch network—only mentions office on Main and drive-thru location
No fee schedule, minimum balance requirements, or product pricing transparency available
Outdated website design and content (welcome message dated October 2016)
No information on loan approval rates, turnaround times, or qualification requirements
best_for: Customers seeking traditional relationship banking with local decision-makers
Multi-generational family members already banking with the institution
Small business owners and agricultural operators needing commercial or farm real estate financing
Community members prioritizing mutual ownership structure over external shareholder models
diagnosis: Century Savings is best for established customers and community members seeking traditional mutual savings and loan banking with local ownership and relationship service. Primary caveat: the institution provides minimal pricing, rate, and product detail online, requiring direct contact for loan terms and deposit rates—unsuitable for customers comparing options based on published rates or seeking immediate digital application processes.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
